target.title: Draft: security: add Security Matrix runtime-fact audit model
target.body:
## Summary

- Refactors the Security Matrix from a trust-source table into a runtime-fact audit model.
- Separates `actor` from external or stored content influence so `agent` is not treated as a trust source.
- Adds `influencedBy`, `approvalState`, and `operatorPolicy` inputs so future runtime consumers can compose the matrix with existing approval and tool-policy boundaries.
- Adds concrete tool-id capability resolution and an inert audit-event builder for future opt-in runtime wiring.
- Updates docs and focused unit coverage for strictest-source evaluation, approval handling, operator-policy denial, and custom-policy weakening guards.

## Why

OpenClaw has multiple trust boundaries where external content can influence later tool decisions. A source/capability table by itself risks becoming a disconnected security authority. This revision keeps the model inert but shapes it around facts a real before-tool-call consumer can supply later: actor, concrete tool id, tool capability, influence sources, approval state, and existing operator policy.

## Scope

This is still model-only and audit-only.

It does not:

- block tool calls
- require confirmation at runtime
- emit runtime diagnostic events
- add config
- change plugin behavior
- change UI
- alter runtime tool execution

This PR now includes an inert `security_matrix.evaluated` audit-event object builder, but nothing emits that event yet.

## Security model

The important behavior change from the first draft is that `agent` and `user` are actors, not trust sources.

An agent-originated tool call with `influencedBy: ["web_fetch"]` and `capability: "exec"` evaluates to `block`. A user-originated tool call with no external influence and an existing operator allow policy can evaluate to `allow`.

Policy overlays cannot weaken default decisions unless `allowPolicyWeakening` is explicitly set. That prevents ordinary custom policy from accidentally changing an externally influenced `exec` decision from `block` to `allow`.

## Validation

Pending after the latest connector-applied patch.

Recommended validation commands:

- `pnpm test src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts`
- `pnpm changed:lanes --base upstream/main --json`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/security/security-matrix.md src/security/security-matrix/default-policy.ts src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.ts src/security/security-matrix/facts.ts src/security/security-matrix/tool-capability.ts src/security/security-matrix/types.ts src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/security/security-matrix/`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/security/security-matrix.md`
- `pnpm exec tsc -p <isolated tsconfig including the security-matrix module>`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm docs:list`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
The evaluator now classifies runtime tool facts instead of treating `agent` or `user` as trust sources. It evaluates concrete capability plus external influence sources, respects existing operator-policy denial, and lets explicit approval satisfy only `require_confirm`, not `block`.

Real environment tested:
Not re-run locally after the latest connector-applied patch. The previous focused validation from `fbfe775d55` is stale for this revision.

Observed result expected from the focused test:

- externally influenced `exec`, `credential_access`, and `system_config` produce `block`
- externally influenced state-changing capabilities produce `require_confirm`
- externally influenced lower-risk read/network/browser/memory-read capabilities produce `warn`
- `agent` plus `web_fetch` plus `exec` produces `block`
- no external influence plus known capability produces `allow`, subject to existing operator policy
- approval can satisfy `require_confirm` but cannot override `block`
- operator-policy denial produces `block`
- custom policy cannot weaken defaults unless explicitly allowed
- concrete tool names such as `exec`, `gmail.send`, and `workspace.apply_patch` map to capabilities
- `createSecurityMatrixAuditEvent` produces an inert `security_matrix.evaluated` object from runtime tool facts

What was not tested:
Runtime hook wiring, runtime audit event emission, runtime enforcement, config modes, CLI display, plugin UI, and live tool execution were not tested because this PR still introduces only the inert model and audit-event object builder.

## AI assistance

This PR was AI-assisted. I reviewed the generated code and understand the model, evaluator, tests, and documentation.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
